areas.summary: The publish direction of the PR review pipeline is structurally sound in its core helpers (single batched review POST, marker-based idempotency, diff anchoring), but every failure transition dead-ends in a log line inside agent.log that nothing reads back: publish success/failure is never persisted to the session row, meta.json, findings.json, or any API, so the operator cannot tell after the fact whether findings reached GitHub — which is exactly the reported top pain. Publishing exists only behind the Workbench's per-run checkbox (default off, no CLI path, the documented repo-config gate was never implemented), launch auto-review findings never enter the publishable findings store at all, and the fence extractor silently truncates findings on a bare ``` opener. State machine as implemented: checkbox→worker→reconcile(fail⇒skip,log-only)→partition(empty diff⇒all out-of-diff)→single POST(fail⇒log-only)→result discarded⇒session 'completed' regardless; crash before finalize⇒'running' forever and 409s all future reviews of that PR.

minor.title: runGh discards stderr unconditionally — publish failures surface as 'unknown' / generic strings, making unreliable posting undiagnosable
minor.detail: runGh collects stderr but never returns it (`void stderr; // captured for diagnostics, discarded on success` — actually discarded always). Auth failures, missing/old gh (e.g. a gh without `--slurp`, used by fetchPublishedFindingIds), spawn errors, and 20s timeouts all settle as {ok:false, stdout:""}. Downstream, postReviewWithInlineComments reports `res.stdout || "gh api reviews POST failed"` and fetchPublishedFindingIds reports `could not fetch existing inline comments: unknown`. The operator debugging 'findings didn't post' gets a one-line log with no HTTP status, no gh error text. Note the timeout default is 20s (gh-pr.ts:68) — a slow paginated comments fetch on a busy PR aborts and skips the publish with the same opaque message.
minor.evidence: gh-pr.ts:125-128 `child.on("close", (code) => { void stderr; // captured for diagnostics, discarded on success\n settle({ stdout: stdout.trim(), ok: code === 0 }); })`; gh-pr-write.ts:137 `return res.ok ? { ok: true } : { ok: false, error: res.stdout || "gh api reviews POST failed" }`; gh-pr-write.ts:311 `could not fetch existing inline comments: ${inlineRes.stdout || "unknown"}`.
minor.fix: Return stderr (trimmed, capped) from runGh — `{ stdout, stderr, ok, timedOut }` — and include it in the error strings of gh-pr-write helpers; distinguish timeout aborts explicitly.
minor.test: Fake gh runner returning ok:false with stderr text; assert publishReviewFindings's log/error includes the stderr detail; timeout case asserts 'timed out after 20000ms' appears.

minor.title: No CLI/headless path runs or publishes a review — `forge review` only prints a prompt; publish exists solely behind a default-off Workbench checkbox; the documented repo-config gate doesn't exist
minor.detail: `forge review <pr>` composes the reviewer prompt and writes it to stdout/--out; it never executes the agent, never parses findings, never publishes — the operator must pipe to claude/codex manually and the output is never captured into findings.json or GitHub. The only publish trigger is the Workbench run-review POST with publishToGitHub:true, and the UI signal defaults to false per page load and is not persisted. ADR-0027 and skills/forge-reviewer/SKILL.md (lines 152-156) document a `repoConfig.publishReviewToGitHub` gate, but grep over src/ finds zero references — the config key was never implemented, so the docs and skill mislead the operator about how to turn publishing on. Net effect: in normal headless operation (launch pipeline or CLI), findings are never published; publishing only happens when the operator manually re-runs a review from the Workbench with the checkbox ticked — friction squarely on the top-pain flow.
minor.evidence: review.ts:23-31 help text: "Compose a reviewer prompt for a PR ... Pipe the output to claude/codex" — run() ends at process.stdout.write(prompt) (review.ts:147); web/signals/review.ts:109 `export const publishToGitHub = signal<boolean>(false);` (not persisted); SKILL.md:154 "repo config `publishReviewToGitHub` **and** the per-review toggle" vs `grep -rn publishReviewToGitHub src/` → no hits (only docs/adr/0027 line 79).
minor.fix: Add `forge review <pr> --run [--publish]` that drives runReviewWorker's logic synchronously (reuse the worker body), and either implement repoConfig.publishReviewToGitHub as a default for the checkbox or fix ADR-0027/SKILL.md to match the per-request-only reality (new superseding ADR note).
minor.test: CLI integration test: `forge review <pr> --run --publish` with stubbed agent + gh runner produces findings.json and one reviews POST; docs lint asserting no src-absent config keys are referenced in SKILL.md.

minor.title: Diff and headRefOid are captured at worker start, minutes before publish — a mid-review push 422s the whole batched review or posts comments on an outdated commit
minor.detail: runReviewWorker fetches prInfoJson (headRefOid) and the diff before invoking the reviewer agent, which typically runs for many minutes. publishFindingsToGitHub then anchors findings against that old diff and POSTs with the old commit_id. If commits were pushed mid-review: (a) GitHub may reject the review (422) because line/side anchors don't exist on the named commit — one rejection kills the entire batched POST, so ALL findings fail to publish and (per pub-publish-outcome-not-persisted) nobody is told; or (b) the review posts but the comments are immediately 'outdated' and collapsed in the GitHub UI — posted into the void. There is no head-moved check or re-fetch before the POST.
minor.evidence: review-actions.ts:357-359 `const prInfoJson = runGhSafe(["pr", "view", ...]); ... const diff = runGhSafe(["pr", "diff", ...])` happen before the agent run (execFileSync at 403); publish uses them at 441-449 (`commitId: headRefOid` via 558-566); gh-pr-write.ts:122-123 `payload.commit_id = args.commitId` with anchors computed from the stale diff (gh-pr-write.ts:379).
minor.fix: Immediately before publishing, re-fetch headRefOid; if it differs from the fetch-time oid, re-fetch the diff and re-partition findings (ids are line-based so most still anchor), or at minimum log + record 'head moved during review' in the publish record.
minor.test: Fake gh runner where the second `pr view` returns a new headRefOid; assert publish re-partitions against the re-fetched diff (or marks the record stale) instead of POSTing old anchors.

minor.title: runGhSafe returns "" on gh failure — a failed `gh pr diff` silently demotes every finding to out-of-diff body bullets (and feeds the reviewer an empty diff)
minor.detail: All three pre-flight gh calls in the worker go through runGhSafe, which logs one line and returns the empty string on any failure. With diff="", the reviewer prompt's Diff section is empty (the agent reviews blind or fetches its own context), and at publish time partitionFindingsByDiff(findings, "") routes every finding to outOfDiff — so instead of inline comments the PR gets a single review body of bullets, with no inline anchoring, and nothing marks the run as degraded. Same for prInfoJson="" (no commit_id, owner/repo re-resolved via `gh repo view` of the cwd, which can disagree with the PR's repo for forks). The run still finalizes 'completed'.
minor.evidence: review-actions.ts:580-594 runGhSafe `catch ... process.stdout.write(...); return "";`; review-actions.ts:357-359 callers take the empty string; diff-anchoring.ts:75-76 `function parseRightSideLines(diff) { if (!diff) return []; }` → partitionFindingsByDiff puts all findings in outOfDiff (243-246).
minor.fix: Treat a failed `gh pr diff`/`gh pr view` as fatal for the publish step (skip publish with a persisted 'diff unavailable' publish record) and degrade the review itself explicitly (record warning in meta/session metrics), instead of silently flattening to body bullets.
minor.test: Worker-level test with gh stub failing `pr diff`: assert publish is skipped with a recorded reason rather than posting a body-only review.

minor.title: Finding id = sha1(file|lineStart|title): line drift across re-reviews defeats marker dedup, producing duplicate comments for the same issue
minor.detail: The idempotency contract keys on ForgeFinding.id, derived from file, lineStart, and title. After a rebase/fix-commit shifts lines, a re-review reports the same issue at a new lineStart (or with slightly different title wording), minting a new id that doesn't match the published marker — so publishReviewFindings posts a second comment for the same underlying issue while the old marker comment lingers (possibly stale-anchored). Dedup works only for byte-identical findings on unchanged lines. Operator-visible as comment churn on iterating PRs.
minor.evidence: reviewer.ts:269-273 `findingId(file, lineStart, title)` sha1 of `${file}|${lineStart}|${title}`; gh-pr-write.ts:381-382 dedup is `inDiff.filter((a) => !published.has(a.finding.id))` — exact id match only; forge-comment-marker.ts:13-15 documents the marker as the store of record.
minor.fix: Fuzzy reconciliation: also treat a finding as published when an existing marker comment has the same file+title (severity), ignoring lineStart; or include a normalized-title-only hash in the marker (v=2) alongside the strict id.
minor.test: Seed an existing marker for sha1(a.ts|10|T); re-publish a finding sha1(a.ts|14|T); assert it is skipped under fuzzy matching.

minor.title: findings.json is a frozen snapshot of the parser at run time — parser fixes never backfill, so review history permanently undercounts findings
minor.detail: Historical ad-hoc runs on disk show review.md with 2-3 `### [` finding headers but findings.json containing 0-1 findings (e.g. 55-s-review-pr-mpt72ge6-3sdma3: 3 headers vs 1 entry; 59-s-review-pr-mpv8s2zc-jpgg4u: 1 header vs 0 entries) — written by an older parser that dropped multi-file Where lines. The current parser handles all of them (verified by re-running parseForgeReviewFindings on the same review.md), but listForgeReviews/loadForgeReview/findLatestForgeFindings serve the stale findings.json verbatim, so those findings remain invisible and unpublishable forever even though review.md has them.
minor.evidence: review-actions.ts:651-660 readReviewFindingsAt reads findings.json only (never re-parses review.md); disk: /Users/tcashel/.forge/runs/pr-review/55-s-review-pr-mpt72ge6-3sdma3/ review.md has 3 `### [` headers, findings.json has 1 entry; current parseForgeReviewFindings on that review.md returns 3.
minor.fix: Either re-parse review.md on read when its header count exceeds findings.json length, or version findings.json with the parser version and lazily re-derive when outdated.
minor.test: Fixture run dir with mismatched review.md/findings.json; assert loadForgeReview returns the re-parsed count.

serious.title: Publish success/failure is never persisted or surfaced — session completes 'completed' even when 0 of N findings posted
serious.detail: runReviewWorker calls publishFindingsToGitHub and discards its outcome entirely. Inside, publishReviewFindings returns a PublishResult {posted, skipped, skippedPost} that the caller never assigns, and every failure path (reconciliation fetch failed, review POST 422/5xx, owner/repo resolution failed) only emits a log line into the detached worker's agent.log. The session is then finalized with state='completed', exitCode=0; nothing writes publish state to the sessions row, meta.json, findings.json, ReviewRunSummary/ReviewRunDetail, or any serve endpoint. Real run dirs under ~/.forge/runs/pr-review/ contain zero '[publish]' lines and no publish field anywhere. Consequence: the operator's top pain verbatim — findings silently fail to post and 'forge status'/Workbench review history show a green completed review. There is also no retry path: the only way to retry a failed publish is to re-run the entire reviewer agent. Violates F0 'no silent failure' for a core flow.
serious.evidence: review-actions.ts:441-449 `await publishFindingsToGitHub({...})` (return value unused); review-actions.ts:575-577 `catch (e) { args.log(`publish to GitHub failed (best-effort): ...`) }`; gh-pr-write.ts:402-404 `if (!res.ok) { log(`[publish] review post failed: ...`); return { posted: 0, ... } }` and gh-pr-write.ts:373-377 `log(`[publish] skipping review post — could not reconcile...`)`; review-actions.ts:459 `const status = exitCode === 0 ? "completed" : "failed"` — exitCode is unaffected by publish; ReviewRunSummary (review-actions.ts:607-617) has no publish field.
serious.fix: Persist a publish record: write `publish.json` (or a `publish` block in meta.json) with {attempted, posted, outOfDiff, skipped, skippedPost, error} from PublishResult, and stamp it into the session metrics blob in finalizeSession. Surface it in listForgeReviews/loadForgeReview and the Workbench review history (badge: published / publish failed / not published). Add a `forge review publish <pr> --session <id>` (or serve POST) that retries from the persisted findings.json without re-running the reviewer.
serious.test: Unit test: stub __setGhRunner so the reviews POST fails; run the publish step via runReviewWorker's helper; assert the session metrics/meta record skippedPost/error and that listForgeReviews exposes it. Second test: successful publish records posted counts.

verdict.correctedDetail: Confirmed: runReviewWorker (review-actions.ts:441-449) awaits publishFindingsToGitHub which returns void; that helper (564-577) discards the PublishResult from publishReviewFindings and catches all errors into a log line. gh-pr-write.ts failure paths (375-376 reconciliation-failed, 403-404 POST-failed) only log and return {posted:0}. Session finalization (459, 473-480) ignores publish outcome entirely; AdHocReviewMeta, ReviewRunSummary (607-617), ReviewRunDetail, and metricsPatch carry no publish field; no serve endpoint exposes publish state; no retry path exists other than re-running the whole review (publishReviewFindings is the sole call site codebase-wide). Two corrections: (1) the failure IS textually visible in agent.log, which is replayable post-hoc via GET /api/sessions/:id/log (serve.ts:1145-1156) — so 'never surfaced' should read 'surfaced only as an unstructured log line, invisible in status/meta/history/findings'; (2) the 'zero [publish] lines in real run dirs' evidence is misleading — grep hits in real logs are quoted source inside reviewer diffs, and the actual worker lines show publish was always skipped by an older repoConfig.publishReviewToGitHub gate, meaning the publish path has never executed in any recorded production run. Session still ends state='completed'/exitCode=0 when 0 of N findings post after the operator opted in — silent failure of the core publish flow per the F0 lens.
verdict.reasoning: Re-traced the full path end to end. review-actions.ts:441 calls publishFindingsToGitHub (returns Promise<void>); inside, publishReviewFindings's PublishResult is unassigned (line 564) and the catch at 575-577 swallows everything into a log callback. gh-pr-write.ts:365-410 returns structured posted/skipped/skippedPost but every failure branch only logs. Finalization at review-actions.ts:459/473-480 derives state solely from the agent exit code; metricsPatch is only sidecar token metrics. Searched the whole repo: publishReviewFindings has exactly one caller; serve.ts has no publish-status or re-publish endpoint; ReviewRunSummary/Detail and meta.json have no publish field; tests pin PublishResult at the library layer only. Checked real runs in ~/.forge/runs/pr-review: apparent '[publish]' matches are quoted source in diffs, and actual worker lines show publish was always skipped by an obsolete config gate — it has never run for real. The only surfacing is the raw agent.log line (replayable via /api/sessions/:id/log), which does not satisfy 'visible in forge status and the Workbench'. Claim is real with minor evidence corrections; BLOCKER stands under the given lens (findings silently not posted in the operator's top-pain flow while status shows completed).

serious.title: extractLastForgeReviewBlock treats a bare ``` opener as a closer — silently drops every finding after it
serious.detail: classifyFence classifies any fence line without an info string as a closing fence. Markdown allows a bare ``` to OPEN a code block, and reviewer agents routinely omit info strings on Evidence snippets. When that happens inside the forge-review block, depth hits 0 at the bare opener and the block is truncated: the current finding loses its Why/Fix and every subsequent finding (plus Verdict-trailing sections) is silently dropped — they never reach findings.json, never display in the Workbench, never publish to GitHub. Reproduced against the current code: a 2-finding review whose first Evidence uses a bare ``` parses to 1 finding with evidence=null. Affects both the ad-hoc worker (review-actions.ts:427) and the launch pipeline's `forge __extract-review` (review-actions.ts:514), where the truncated review.md also feeds the auto-fixer. Historical artifacts confirm drops happen in practice: ~/.forge/runs/pr-review/55-s-review-pr-mpt72ge6-3sdma3 has 3 `### [` headers in review.md but only 1 finding in findings.json (older parser bug, same silent-drop class, never backfilled).
serious.evidence:
reviewer.ts:150-154 `function classifyFence(line) { ... return { fence: true, opening: m[2].trim().length > 0 }; }` and reviewer.ts:191-196 `} else if (f.fence) { depth--; ... }` — bare fence always decrements. Repro (bun -e against src/core/reviewer.ts): block with `**Evidence:**
```
const x = 1;
``` truncates after `**Evidence:**`, parseForgeReviewFindings returns only ["first finding"].
serious.fix: Track open-fence pairing instead of pure depth: remember the opening fence's marker length/char; at depth 1, only a bare fence whose marker matches the outer ```forge-review opener closes the block when the line is followed by EOF/non-block content — simpler: when at depth 1 and a bare fence is seen, look ahead — if another bare fence exists before the next ```forge-review/EOF, treat the pair as a nested block. Alternatively instruct + enforce: take the text from the forge-review opener to the LAST bare fence in the document (the reviewer emits the block last per the skill).
serious.test: Add to tests/core/reviewer.test.ts: a forge-review block whose Evidence uses a bare ``` opener; assert both findings parse and the second finding's Why/Fix survive.

verdict.correctedDetail: The core mechanic is real and I reproduced it against current code: classifyFence (src/core/reviewer.ts:150-154) treats any fence line without an info string as a closer, so a bare ``` that OPENS a nested Evidence block at depth 1 drives depth to 0 in extractLastForgeReviewBlock (reviewer.ts:186-201), truncating the forge-review block mid-finding. My repro (bun -e, 2-finding review, first Evidence opened with bare ```) yields 1 finding with evidence=null/why=""/fix=""; the second finding is silently dropped. Both call sites are unguarded: the ad-hoc worker (src/cli/cmd/review-actions.ts:427-435) writes the truncated block to review.md/findings.json and publishes only the surviving findings; `forge __extract-review` (review-actions.ts:514, invoked from src/core/launch.ts:217 and :676) feeds the same truncated review.md to the auto-fixer. tests/core/reviewer.test.ts:144 only covers nested fences WITH info strings, so nothing pins the bare-opener case. However, two supporting claims are overstated: (1) "routinely" — I ran the current extractor over all 8 real review-raw.md artifacts in ~/.forge/runs/pr-review and every extracted block is complete (ends with "## What I Skipped"); zero real-world truncations exist on disk, and the reviewer SKILL.md template models ```ts-style openers. (2) The historical artifact 55-s-review-pr-mpt72ge6-3sdma3 does NOT confirm this bug class fires in practice — its raw parses to 3 findings under current code; the 1-finding findings.json came from a different, already-fixed parser bug. So this is a real latent silent-drop hazard contingent on reviewer output style, not a demonstrated recurring failure; HIGH (not BLOCKER) is the right severity.
verdict.reasoning: Re-traced the cited path and independently reproduced the truncation with bun -e against src/core/reviewer.ts: a bare ``` opener inside Evidence terminates the forge-review block (depth 1->0 at reviewer.ts:192-196), dropping all subsequent findings and the current finding's Why/Fix. Verified both callers (review-actions.ts:427 ad-hoc worker writing findings.json and publishing to GitHub; review-actions.ts:514 __extract-review used by launch.ts:217/676 feeding the fixer) have no truncation guard — only a null-block check, and a truncated block is non-null. Verified no test covers bare nested openers (reviewer.test.ts:144 uses ```text). Checked for false-positive escape hatches: no header-count cross-check, no warning path, code not dead. Counter-evidence on frequency: all 8 real review-raw.md artifacts extract completely under current code, and the auditor's historical artifact is explained by a different fixed bug, so the "routinely"/"confirmed in practice" framing is overstated; the bug is latent, triggered only by reviewer output that omits info strings, which is valid markdown and plausible but not yet observed in stored runs.

serious.title: Launch auto-review findings are never parsed to findings.json — the documented launch bucket of findLatestForgeFindings is dead code, so pipeline review findings are invisible and unpublishable
serious.detail: findLatestForgeFindings documents two sources: ad-hoc (`<runsDir>/pr-review/...`) and launch auto-review (`<runsDir>/<task-id>/findings.json` filtered by branch). But nothing ever writes findings.json for a launch run: parseForgeReviewFindings is invoked only in the ad-hoc worker, and the bash runner only extracts review.md + verdict via `forge __extract-review` (which writes the block and prints the verdict, never findings). Verified on disk: every launch run dir (~/.forge/runs/<plan-id>/) has review.md but no findings.json; grep for findings.json writers hits only review-actions.ts. Consequence: for the core launch→review→PR pipeline, the Workbench review bundle's forgeFindings is always empty (unless an ad-hoc review was separately run), so launch reviewer findings can never be displayed as structured findings, never selected for comment-fix by id, and never published to GitHub. The operator sees 'request-changes' verdicts (meta.json) with no findings anywhere except raw review.md text.
serious.evidence: review-actions.ts:792-799 comment: "2) <runsDir>/<task-id>/findings.json (launch auto-review)" and 829-845 the launch candidate loop; review-actions.ts:433-434 is the ONLY findings.json writer (`fs.writeFileSync(path.join(runDir, "findings.json"), ...)` in the ad-hoc worker); runExtractReviewBlock (review-actions.ts:501-519) writes only the block + verdict. Disk: /Users/tcashel/.forge/runs/feat-review-publish-pr-findings-to-g-mpulaed1/ contains review.md, review-raw.md but no findings.json.
serious.fix: In runExtractReviewBlock (already invoked by every launch runner), also parse findings from the extracted block and write findings.json next to review.md: `fs.writeFileSync(outDir/findings.json, JSON.stringify(parseForgeReviewFindings(block)))`. That single change lights up the existing launch bucket in findLatestForgeFindings, the review bundle, comment-fix, and (checkbox permitting) publish.
serious.test: Run `forge __extract-review` on a fixture raw output containing findings; assert findings.json appears beside the out file and findLatestForgeFindings returns it with source='launch'.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed by full re-trace. (1) Reader: findLatestForgeFindings (src/cli/cmd/review-actions.ts:792-845) implements a documented launch bucket reading <runsDir>/<plan-id>/findings.json filtered by plan.branch === headRefName. (2) Writer: the ONLY findings.json writer in the repo is review-actions.ts:434 inside the ad-hoc __review-worker, whose runDir is <runsDir>/pr-review/<prNum>-<sessionId> (the ad-hoc bucket). (3) Launch path: src/core/launch.ts:217 and :676 call only `forge __extract-review`; runExtractReviewBlock (review-actions.ts:501-519) writes the forge-review block to review.md and prints the verdict — it never parses or writes findings. grep of launch.ts for "findings" hits only prompt text. (4) Never existed: `git log -S findings.json -- src/core/launch.ts` is empty; the bucket comment shipped in f87398b alongside the ad-hoc feature with no launch writer. No test references findLatestForgeFindings; test fixtures writing findings.json (tests/review-actions.test.ts:206, tests/serve-review-bundle-dedup.test.ts:115-169) seed only pr-review/ dirs. (5) Disk: all three launch runs in ~/.forge/runs with review.md (e.g. feat-review-publish-pr-findings-to-g-mpulaed1) lack findings.json. (6) Consequences verified: serve.ts:1208 builds the bundle's forgeFindings solely from this lookup; comment-fix-actions.ts:285/:569 gate fix-by-finding-id on it; publishFindingsToGitHub (review-actions.ts:440-450) is called only inline in the ad-hoc worker. So launch auto-review findings are never structured, never selectable for comment-fix by id, and never publishable to GitHub — only the verdict (meta.json reviewVerdict, set at launch.ts:222-231/681-695) and raw review.md survive. Refutation attempts (hidden writer, review.md fallback parser, pinning tests) all came up empty: parseForgeReviewFindings has exactly one caller (review-actions.ts:433). Severity HIGH is correct: directly hits the operator's top pain (findings not reliably posted to GitHub) for the core launch pipeline, but the ad-hoc review path provides a workaround and the verdict remains visible, so it falls short of BLOCKER's "findings dropped/invisible" (the raw review text still exists on disk).

serious.title: No reaper for purpose='review' sessions — a worker killed before finalizeSession leaves 'running' forever and 409-blocks all future reviews of that PR
serious.detail: runAdHocReview's single-flight guard rejects with REVIEW_IN_FLIGHT whenever any sessions row has purpose='review', state='running' for the (prNum, repoRoot). The detached worker is the only thing that flips that row to a terminal state (finalizeSession at the end of runReviewWorker). If the worker dies without reaching it — SIGKILL (a real prior run shows the reviewer agent killed with 137), machine sleep/reboot, bun crash, OOM — the row stays 'running' forever. reconcileExecutionSessions only sweeps purpose='execution' sessions (and only via job-row joins, which review sessions don't have). Consequence: the PR can never be ad-hoc-reviewed again from the Workbench (409 on every attempt), review history permanently shows a phantom 'running' run (lying status), and there is no CLI/UI recovery path short of hand-editing the SQLite DB in ~/.forge.
serious.evidence: review-actions.ts:170-186 in-flight guard `WHERE purpose = 'review' AND state = 'running' ... LIMIT 1` → `throw new CliError("REVIEW_IN_FLIGHT", ...)`; writes.ts:212-225 `reconcileExecutionSessions` — `WHERE sessions.purpose = 'execution'` only; review sessions have no pid recorded and no watchdog (upsertSession at review-actions.ts:214-231 stores only logFile/runDir/prNum/repoRoot/publishToGitHub in metrics).
serious.fix: Record the worker pid in session metrics at spawn; in the in-flight guard (and listForgeReviews), treat a 'running' review session as stale when the pid is dead (process.kill(pid,0) fails) or startedAt exceeds a TTL, finalize it as failed('worker died'), and proceed. Optionally add a 'Cancel stuck review' action in the Workbench.
serious.test: Insert a sessions row purpose='review' state='running' with a dead pid; call runAdHocReview for the same (prNum, repoRoot); assert it finalizes the stale row and starts a new review instead of throwing REVIEW_IN_FLIGHT.

verdict.correctedDetail: Confirmed: the single-flight guard (review-actions.ts:170-186) matches any purpose='review', state='running' row for (prNum, repoRoot) with no age bound or liveness check, and the detached worker is the only post-spawn finalizer (finalizeSession at review-actions.ts:473; the parent finalizes only on spawn failure at :264). reconcileExecutionSessions (writes.ts:212-225) filters purpose='execution' and joins jobs.session_id — review sessions have related_id=null and no jobs row, so they are never swept; boot reapers (reapStaleCritiques, reapStalePlanChats in serve.ts:2673/2713) don't cover them; the SSE emitDoneFromSession (serve.ts:636-656) is read-only; no pid is stored (review-actions.ts:214-231 omits pid); listForgeReviews shows the phantom row as 'running' forever; no kill endpoint exists for review sessions. Two corrections: (1) the '137' evidence is the wrong trigger — if the reviewer AGENT exits 137, the worker's catch (review-actions.ts:451-455) still finalizes the row as failed; the orphan only occurs when the worker PROCESS itself dies (reboot, OOM, kill -9, bun crash). (2) Recovery does not require hand-editing SQLite: the undocumented plumbing command `forge session finish --id <sid> --exit-code 1` (session.ts:102-148) finalizes any session row, and the 409 message includes the stuck session id — but there is no designed Workbench/CLI recovery path an operator would discover.
verdict.reasoning: Re-traced every cited path plus all potential refutations. The guard query and 409 mapping (serve.ts:2311) are exact. Searched the entire codebase for session-state writers: only finalizeSession, upsertSession, updateCritiqueSession (critique-only), and reconcileExecutionSessions (execution-only, jobs-join) exist — no reaper, boot sweep, kill endpoint, or SSE-side finalize covers purpose='review' rows. A worker killed before finalizeSession therefore leaves state='running' permanently, 409-blocking all future ad-hoc reviews of that PR and showing a phantom 'running' run in review history. The claim is real as described, with two mis-statements: the 137-exit-of-the-agent scenario is actually handled (worker catch finalizes failed; only worker-process death orphans the row), and `forge session finish` is an existing albeit undocumented CLI escape hatch, so 'hand-edit SQLite' overstates the recovery cost. HIGH (lying status, blocked core review flow) rather than BLOCKER (no findings lost) is correct.

serious.title: Generated runner's set_status and unquoted set_meta_field calls are silent no-ops — failure statuses, baseSha/finalSha, and the reviewer-crash reviewError never reach meta.json
serious.detail: In the bash emitted by generateRunnerScript, `set_status() { set_meta_field "status" ""$1""; }` — in shell, `""$1""` is just `$1` (empty string + value + empty string), so python receives a bare token like `reviewing` and `json.loads('reviewing')` raises; the helper's `2>/dev/null || true` swallows it. Every set_status transition (running, quality_check, quality_failed, failed×3, creating_pr, reviewing, fixing, done) and the `""$BASE_SHA""`/`""$FINAL_SHA""`/`""reviewer process exited with code $REVIEWER_EXIT""` writes silently no-op. Confirmed empirically: every meta.json under ~/.forge/runs has baseSha/finalSha = null, and a reviewer-process crash records no reviewError. Status only changes when a separate inline python block runs (PR-created→'done') or the TS kill path writes 'failed'. Consequence for the review pipeline: a reviewer agent that exits non-zero in the launch flow leaves meta.json with no reviewError and a stale status — 'forge status' and the Workbench lie about the terminal state (F0 'no silent failure' violated); a failure before PR creation leaves status stuck at the last python-written value.
serious.evidence: launch.ts:414 `set_status() { set_meta_field "status" ""$1""; }`; launch.ts:438 `set_meta_field "baseSha" ""$BASE_SHA""`; launch.ts:696 `set_meta_field "reviewError" ""reviewer process exited with code $REVIEWER_EXIT""`; set_meta_field (launch.ts:403-412) does `json.loads(val_json)` under `2>/dev/null || true`. Shell repro: `set_status reviewing` → python json.loads fails → no-op; `"\"approve\""` parses. Disk proof: all four /Users/tcashel/.forge/runs/*/meta.json have baseSha=null, finalSha=null; generated run.sh line 49 shows the same broken quoting.
serious.fix: Quote correctly: `set_status() { set_meta_field "status" "\"$1\""; }` and `set_meta_field "baseSha" "\"$BASE_SHA\""` etc. (or have set_meta_field json-encode sys.argv[2] itself with a --raw flag for pre-encoded values). Also drop the `2>/dev/null || true` swallow in favor of appending to session-helper.log.
serious.test: Generate a runner script via generateRunnerScript, source its set_meta_field/set_status into a bash test harness with a temp meta.json, call set_status failed, assert meta.json status becomes 'failed' and baseSha gets written.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed by direct code read, on-disk generated artifact, and empirical repro. launch.ts:403-412 set_meta_field runs python json.loads(argv[2]) with `2>/dev/null || true`, so non-JSON args silently no-op. launch.ts:414 emits `set_status() { set_meta_field "status" ""$1""; }` — in bash `""$1""` is empty-string+value+empty-string, a bare token; json.loads('running') raises and is swallowed. The generated runner on disk (~/.forge/runs/plan-library-in-the-workbench-mpvfu6p9/run.sh:49,80,193,355) contains the identical broken quoting, and I reproduced the no-op with the exact helper (broken calls left meta unchanged; the single-quoted-JSON form '"approve"' succeeded). All four real run meta.json files have baseSha=null and finalSha=null despite reaching PR creation, corroborating launch.ts:438/534 no-ops. reviewVerdict works only because __extract-review (review-actions.ts:517) prints JSON.stringify(verdict) with quotes, and the literal `"null"` / single-quoted '"..."' calls are valid JSON. launch.ts:696's reviewError additionally word-splits ($2 becomes `reviewer`) — no-op either way, so a reviewer-process crash records no reviewError. I searched for compensating paths: the only meta.json status writers are the TS launch seed status:"running" (launch.ts:831-839), the inline-python done block gated on PR_URL (launch.ts:608-618), and the kill handler (serve.ts:2551). No tmux-liveness reconciler exists; store.syncPlanStatus (store.ts:399-419) propagates meta.status to forge status/Workbench, so an agent/quality/PR-creation failure leaves the plan showing "running" forever, and status flips to "done" at PR creation before review/auto-fix run. tests/gh.test.ts:45 only asserts the string `set_status "failed"` exists in generated output — it does not execute it.
verdict.correctedDetail: Claim is accurate with two minor refinements: (1) a failure before PR creation leaves status stuck at the TS-seeded "running" (launch.ts:831), not a python-written value — i.e. failed runs appear running forever in forge status/Workbench until manually killed; additionally status flips to "done" at PR creation (launch.ts:611) while reviewing/fixing are still in progress, so the in-review phases are also misreported. (2) `set_meta_field "reviewVerdict" "null"` calls DO succeed (bare `null` is valid JSON), so on reviewer crash the verdict is correctly nulled — only the line-696 reviewError message is silently dropped.

areas.summary: The resolve/re-review direction of the PR review pipeline is structurally sound (stable source:id tokens, marker-based dedup, paginated+slurped reconciliation before publish, best-effort GitHub writes that never corrupt local state) but it leaks failures silently at almost every boundary. The worst gaps: a publish failure or skip leaves the review session green with no record anywhere (the operator's exact top pain), fetchPrBundle still has the un-slurped pagination bug that gh-pr-write already fixed (breaking comment targeting on busy PRs), the lineStart-hashed finding id defeats dedup the moment a fix commit shifts lines, and a killed worker leaves a 'running' session that permanently 409-blocks re-runs with no reaper or kill route. The headless trap also exists verbatim: the fixer's bare `git commit`/`git push` will hang on 1Password gpg signing or credential prompts.

minor.title: validation.json stamps targets 'fixed' before quality gates/commit run; a crash mid-gate leaves meta.status='running' and the UI reports fixed work that was never committed
minor.detail: The worker writes validation.json with status='fixed' for all valid verdicts immediately after parsing (optimistically), then runs quality gates and only re-stamps to 'failed' on a gate failure it survives. findLatestCommentFixState demotes fixed→failed only when meta.status === 'failed' — but a worker killed during the (long) quality-gate or commit phase never rewrites meta.json, which still says status='running'. Result: the Review UI permanently shows those targets as fixed (commentFixState entries 'fixed') while no commit exists and the worktree may hold uncommitted agent edits, which will then trip the WORKTREE_DIRTY assert on the next run.
minor.evidence: comment-fix-actions.ts:704-712: `validationEntries.push({ ...v, status: v.verdict === "valid" ? "fixed" : "disputed" }); writeValidation(runDir, validationEntries);` happens before runQualityGates (729) and commitAndPush (745). findLatestCommentFixState 1311: `const sessionFailed = meta.status === "failed";` 1330-1331: `if (sessionFailed && status === "fixed") status = "failed";` — meta stuck at 'running' (seed meta, line 345) bypasses the demotion.
minor.fix: Stamp valid rows as a provisional status (e.g. 'pending') until after commit+push, or treat meta.status='running' older than a TTL (or with a dead session) the same as failed when projecting commentFixState.
minor.test: Write a run dir with validation.json entries status='fixed' and meta.json status='running' plus a finished/failed session; assert findLatestCommentFixState does not report 'fixed'.

minor.title: A finding validated 'valid' with zero file edits (already fixed, e.g. after a crash-after-push or prior partial run) can never get its GitHub thread resolved by Forge
minor.detail: Thread resolution is gated on committedAndPushed. If the agent validates a finding as valid but makes no edits — the common state after a previous worker pushed the fix but died before resolvePublishedFindingThreads, or when the code was fixed manually — changedFiles is 0, committedAndPushed stays false, and resolve is skipped ('fixed but nothing was pushed — leaving thread open'). Re-running the fixer reproduces the same state every time, so the published thread stays open forever and the operator must resolve it by hand on GitHub. There is no 'resolve thread' affordance in Forge for this case. The gate is correct for never-resolving-without-a-fix, but it has no escape hatch for fixes that already landed.
minor.evidence: comment-fix-actions.ts:721-726: `if (validCount === 0 || changedFiles.length === 0) { ... qualityResult = { ok: true, ... } }` → committedAndPushed remains false; 1230-1233: `if (!committedAndPushed) { log(`finding ${target.id} fixed but nothing was pushed — leaving thread open`); continue; }`. Behavior pinned by tests/comment-fix-resolve.test.ts:101-122 ('leaves a fixed thread open when nothing was pushed').
minor.fix: When status='fixed' and changedFiles===0, check whether the finding's anchor already reflects the fix (or simply allow resolution when the head commit differs from the comment's commitId), or add a manual 'mark resolved on GitHub' action in the Review UI backed by resolveReviewThread.
minor.test: Worker run where agent emits valid verdicts but no edits, with a published thread present; assert either the thread resolves or the UI exposes an explicit unresolved-but-fixed state with a resolve action.

minor.title: Out-of-diff findings are published only as review-body bullets — resolve-on-fix can never resolve or reply to them, and the skip is just a log line
minor.detail: publishReviewFindings posts out-of-diff findings as bullets (with markers) in the review summary body, not as inline comments, so they have no review thread. resolvePublishedFindingThreads builds its published map exclusively from review-thread comments, so a fixed/disputed out-of-diff finding hits 'finding X has no published thread on the PR — skipping': no resolution, no dispute reply, no edit of the review body. ghResolved is never set, so the GitHub side permanently shows the finding as outstanding even though Forge committed the fix. Re-publish dedup does still suppress re-posting (markers in review bodies are scanned by fetchPublishedFindingIds), so the failure is purely on the resolution/visibility side.
minor.evidence: gh-pr-write.ts:340-350 buildReviewBodySummary embeds markers in the review body for outOfDiff findings. comment-fix-actions.ts:1207-1215: published map built only from `fetchReviewThreads` comments; 1224-1227: `const hit = published.get(target.id); if (!hit) { log(`finding ${target.id} has no published thread on the PR — skipping`); continue; }`.
minor.fix: On fix of an out-of-diff finding, post a PR-level reply (issue comment or review-body edit appending '✅ fixed in <sha>') keyed by the marker, and persist that as the entry's GitHub write status instead of silently skipping.
minor.test: resolvePublishedFindingThreads with entries for a finding whose marker exists only in a review body (no thread); assert a visible GitHub write occurs (or the entry records an explicit 'unresolvable: published out-of-diff' status), not just a log line.

minor.title: findLatestCommentFixState surfaces only the single newest run's validation.json — fixed/disputed badges from earlier fix runs vanish after any later run
minor.detail: The projection picks the newest validation.json by mtime and returns only its entries. A second fix run targeting different comments erases the first run's fixed/disputed/ghResolved state from the bundle (commentFixState), so rows the operator already fixed lose their badges. Thread isResolved from GitHub remains ground truth for published findings whose threads were actually resolved, but `disputed` reasons and `failed` markers for comment:/review: targets have no GitHub fallback and simply disappear.
minor.evidence: comment-fix-actions.ts:1276-1339 findLatestCommentFixState: `candidates.sort((a, b) => b.mtime - a.mtime); for (const c of candidates) { ... return out; }` — returns on the FIRST (newest) matching run dir; earlier runs' entries are never merged.
minor.fix: Merge entries across run dirs newest-wins per targetId instead of returning the newest file wholesale.
minor.test: Two run dirs for the same PR with disjoint targets; assert findLatestCommentFixState contains both runs' entries with newest-wins on overlap.

minor.title: fetchReviewThreads caps each thread at comments(first:50) with no inner pagination — markers past 50 replies become invisible
minor.detail: Thread pagination is handled (100/page, 50-page ceiling), but the nested comments connection fetches only the first 50 comments per thread and never pages. Forge's marker is normally the thread's first comment so this rarely bites for resolve-on-fix, but the bundle's threadByCommentId mapping (serve.ts) and the published map would miss any comment beyond 50 in a long human discussion thread, e.g. a reply chain on a Forge finding.
minor.evidence: gh-pr-write.ts:146-159 REVIEW_THREADS_QUERY: `comments(first:50){ nodes{ databaseId body } }` — no pageInfo/cursor on the inner connection; fetchReviewThreads (183-233) pages only the outer reviewThreads connection.
minor.fix: Either raise first: to 100 and accept the cap with a comment, or request pageInfo on the inner connection and fetch overflow threads individually.
minor.test: Fake GraphQL response with a 60-comment thread whose marker comment is at index 55; assert the marker is still found (after fix).

serious.title: Publish-to-GitHub result is discarded: a failed or skipped review POST still ends the review session 'completed' with no structured record
serious.detail: When the operator checks 'Publish to PR', the review worker calls publishReviewFindings best-effort. If reconciliation fails (any gh hiccup fetching existing comments/reviews) the POST is skipped entirely (skippedPost), and if the review POST itself fails it is only logged. In every case the PublishResult is thrown away: nothing in meta.json, findings.json, or session metrics records whether/what was posted. The session finalizes state='completed' exitCode=0, so 'forge status' and the Workbench show a green review while zero findings reached GitHub. This is the exact operator top pain ('PR review pipeline does NOT reliably post findings to GitHub') and a direct 'no silent failure' violation — the only trace is a log line inside agent.log.
serious.evidence: review-actions.ts:440-450: `if (shouldPublishToGitHub(metrics) && findings.length > 0) { await publishFindingsToGitHub({...})}` — return value ignored; review-actions.ts:564-577: `await publishReviewFindings(...)` result discarded, catch logs `publish to GitHub failed (best-effort)`. gh-pr-write.ts:373-377: `log('[publish] skipping review post — could not reconcile...'); return { posted: 0, ..., skippedPost: true };` and 402-405: `log('[publish] review post failed...'); return {...}`. AdHocReviewMeta (review-actions.ts:59-70) has no publish fields; finalizeSession at 473-480 records only exitCode/error.
serious.fix: Persist PublishResult (posted/outOfDiff/skipped/skippedPost + error) into meta.json and session metrics; surface a 'published N/M findings' or 'PUBLISH FAILED' badge in the review run summary (listForgeReviews) and forge status. Optionally finalize the session as 'completed-with-warnings' or set a non-null error when publish was requested but posted 0 due to failure.
serious.test: Unit test runReviewWorker with a stubbed ghRunner that fails the reviews POST; assert meta.json/session metrics record publish failure and the /api/prs/:num/reviews summary exposes it.

verdict.reasoning: Every cited line checks out on re-trace. publishReviewFindings (src/core/gh-pr-write.ts:365-410) returns a PublishResult whose skip/failure branches (371-377 reconciliation skip with skippedPost:true; 402-404 POST failure) only emit log lines; the module never throws by design. Its sole caller, publishFindingsToGitHub (src/cli/cmd/review-actions.ts:544-578), returns Promise<void>, discards the result, and its catch reduces any error to a log line. The worker (440-450) therefore proceeds with error=null/exitCode=0; AdHocReviewMeta (59-70) has no publish fields, writeMetaSafe (460-471) and finalizeSession (473-480) record only status/exitCode/token-metrics. Read surfaces — listForgeReviews/loadForgeReview (701-783), the serve.ts review bundle, and ReviewActionBar.tsx — show a completed green review with local finding counts and carry zero publish-outcome state. tests/core/gh-pr-write.test.ts pins skippedPost values at the helper level, confirming the result shape exists for callers to consume, yet the only caller drops it. No guard, alternate surfacing path, or test refutes the claim; the only trace of a failed/skipped publish is the [publish] line inside agent.log. The behavior is intentionally 'best-effort' per code comments, but that does not surface it. Minor nuance: findings are not lost (findings.json persists and renders locally) and the log line streams via SSE if watched, so it is a silent partial failure rather than data loss — BLOCKER still holds under the stated lens ('silent failure of a core flow'), since publish-to-GitHub is the operator's named top-pain flow and recovery requires rerunning the entire review with no signal that it is needed.
verdict.correctedDetail: Claim is accurate as stated. One precision: findings are never lost locally (findings.json persists and the Workbench bundle/history show them), and the publish-failure log line is visible in the live SSE log drawer — so the failure is 'unsurfaced in all structured/terminal-state surfaces' rather than totally invisible. Also note the gap is structural: PublishResult exists and is test-pinned specifically so callers can act on it, but the single caller (publishFindingsToGitHub, review-actions.ts:544-578) is typed Promise<void> and discards it.

serious.title: fetchPrBundle uses `gh api --paginate` without `--slurp` — PRs with >100 inline comments/reviews fail JSON.parse, dropping all comments and breaking fixer targeting
serious.detail: gh-pr-write.ts was already fixed for this exact bug ('Without --slurp a >1-page response is concatenated JSON documents that JSON.parse rejects — which previously hit the parse-error path and skipped publishing on busy PRs'), but fetchPrBundle still parses raw --paginate output. On a PR with >100 inline comments (busy PRs, precisely the ones being fixed), JSON.parse throws, inlineComments degrades to [] with a warning. Consequences: (1) runCommentFix's anchoredCommentIds is empty so every `comment:` target is rejected ('None of the supplied targets match...'); (2) the worker's inlineById is empty so already-accepted comment targets are stamped disputed with the misleading reason 'target no longer present on the PR'; (3) review-bundle marker dedup (serve.ts:1227) parses no markers, so published Forge findings reappear as unpublished local finding rows and resolve-on-fix linkage in the UI is lost. Same flaw on issues/comments and pulls/reviews fetches.
serious.evidence: gh-pr.ts:365-371: `runGh(["api", `repos/.../pulls/${prNum}/comments`, "--paginate", ...hostArgs])` then `JSON.parse(inlineRes.stdout) as RawInlineComment[]` (same at 397-403 and 419-425). Contrast gh-pr-write.ts:302-307 comment + use of `--paginate --slurp` + flattenSlurpedPages. Downstream: comment-fix-actions.ts:280-282 `anchoredCommentIds = new Set(bundle.bundle.inlineComments.filter(...))`; 576-580 `const c = inlineById.get(Number(t.id)); if (!c) { unresolved.add(token); }` → 699 reason 'target no longer present on the PR'.
serious.fix: Add `--slurp` to the three paginated gh api calls in fetchPrBundle and reuse flattenSlurpedPages (export it from gh-pr-write.ts or move to gh-pr.ts).
serious.test: Feed fetchPrBundle a fake runGh returning two-page slurped output (`[[...],[...]]`) and legacy concatenated output; assert all comments parse and a 150-comment PR yields 150 inlineComments.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed by reading every cited path end to end. (1) Premise: /Users/tcashel/repositories/forge/src/core/gh-pr.ts:365-371, 397-403, 419-425 all run `gh api ... --paginate` (no `--slurp`) and feed raw stdout to JSON.parse. runGh (gh-pr.ts:67-105) is a bare spawn of gh with no page post-processing. The repo's own gh-pr-write.ts:63-69 comment documents exactly why this fails: without --slurp 'a multi-page response is ... a single concatenated/invalid JSON blob' — gh emits one JSON array per page back-to-back ([...][...]), which JSON.parse rejects; gh-pr-write.ts:302-323 was already converted to `--paginate --slurp` + flattenSlurpedPages, while fetchPrBundle was not (grep confirms gh-pr.ts is the only remaining --paginate-without-slurp site). gh api --paginate sets per_page=100 for REST, so the >100-item threshold is correct. (2) On parse failure the catch at gh-pr.ts:390-392 degrades to a warnings entry and inlineComments stays []. (3) Downstream consequence A confirmed: comment-fix-actions.ts:280-282 builds anchoredCommentIds from bundle.bundle.inlineComments; with [] every `comment:` target is filtered at :290, and an all-comment request throws NO_COMMENTS 'None of the supplied targets match...' at :295-301 — a loud but misleading failure. (4) Consequence B confirmed: worker re-fetch at :555-559, inlineById from inlineComments at :566; missing ids go into `unresolved` (:577-580) and are stamped disputed with reason 'target no longer present on the PR' (:697-701) — lying terminal state persisted to validation.json. (5) Consequence C confirmed: serve.ts:1225-1237 iterates result.bundle.inlineComments to parse forge-finding markers; with [] markerByCommentId/publishedIds stay empty, so published findings are not de-duped (reappear as unpublished local rows) and thread/resolution enrichment at :1239-1268 never attaches — resolve-on-fix linkage lost in the Review UI. No test pins fetchPrBundle pagination (only tests/core/gh-pr-write.test.ts exists). Severity HIGH is right rather than BLOCKER: the degradation is not fully silent (warnings pushed into the bundle; comment-fix start fails with an explicit if misleading CliError), and findings are not destroyed — but it breaks fixer targeting precisely on busy PRs and records false 'disputed/no longer present' states.
verdict.correctedDetail: Claim is accurate as stated. One precision: the comment-fix start path (comment-fix-actions.ts:295-301) only hard-fails when ALL supplied targets are comment-sourced; mixed requests proceed with comment targets silently dropped at the filter (:289-294), which is arguably worse. Also the parse failure does surface as a bundle warning (gh-pr.ts:391), so it is degraded-with-warning rather than fully silent.

serious.title: Finding id hashes lineStart, so re-review after a fix commit re-posts duplicate comments for any finding whose line moved
serious.detail: ForgeFinding.id = sha1(`file|lineStart|title`). The publish dedup (fetchPublishedFindingIds → marker ids) only suppresses exact-id matches. After the fixer pushes a commit (or any new commit shifts lines), a re-review of the PR reports the same remaining findings at shifted lineStart values → new ids → publishReviewFindings treats them as unpublished and posts duplicate inline comments next to the old (possibly resolved) threads. It also severs the resolve linkage: the old marker comment's id is no longer in the latest findings.json, so the marker-suppression in review-bundle stops suppressing (publishedIds vs findings mismatch) and a `finding:<oldId>` selection no longer matches findLatestForgeFindings (see separate silent-drop finding). The marker contract doc claims 'publishing stays idempotent', but it is only idempotent when no line shifts between runs — the common case after an auto-fix round is exactly a line shift.
serious.evidence: reviewer.ts:269-273: `function findingId(file, lineStart, title) { h.update(`${file}|${lineStart}|${title}`)... }`; gh-pr-write.ts:381-383: `const newInDiff = inDiff.filter((a) => !published.has(a.finding.id));` — dedup is exact-id only. forge-comment-marker.ts:10-15: 're-running a review parses markers ... so publishing stays idempotent'.
serious.fix: Make dedup location-tolerant: either drop lineStart from the id (file|title, with a disambiguation counter for same-title-same-file), or at publish time fuzzy-match a new finding against published markers by (file, title) and skip/update instead of re-posting.
serious.test: Publish finding at file.ts:10; re-run publishReviewFindings with the same finding parsed at file.ts:13 (same title/file); assert no second comment is posted.

verdict.correctedDetail: Mechanism confirmed: reviewer.ts:269-273 computes `findingId` as sha1(`${file}|${lineStart}|${title}`).slice(0,12); gh-pr-write.ts:381-382 dedups strictly by exact id against marker-derived ids (`inDiff.filter((a) => !published.has(a.finding.id))`) with no fuzzy file+title fallback anywhere; forge-comment-marker.ts:10-15 indeed promises "publishing stays idempotent". So a re-review whose remaining findings report shifted lineStart values gets new ids and re-posts duplicate inline comments, and the review-bundle suppression (serve.ts:1226-1270, `publishedIds.has(f.id)`) likewise stops matching old marker ids against the latest findings.json. However, two corrections: (1) The "after an auto-fix round" framing is wrong for the built-in pipeline — the launch auto-fix re-review loop (launch.ts:178-238) never publishes to GitHub at all; it only extracts a verdict into review.md, and grep confirms launch.ts contains no findings.json write or publishReviewFindings call. Publishing happens only in operator-initiated ad-hoc reviews gated on the "Publish to PR" opt-in (review-actions.ts:438-451). The duplicate-posting scenario is: publish-enabled ad-hoc review → any line-shifting commit (e.g. Workbench comment-fix push) → second publish-enabled ad-hoc review. (2) Line shift is only one of two id-instability sources — the title is LLM-generated free text, so even without line movement a re-review can re-phrase titles and produce new ids; cross-run dedup is inherently best-effort, and the marker doc's idempotency claim only truly holds for re-publishing the same findings.json. Impact is duplicate visible comments and severed marker/finding linkage — noise and confusion, not dropped or invisible findings, so MEDIUM under the stated lens (friction in the review pipeline, the operator's top pain) rather than HIGH.
verdict.reasoning: Re-traced every cited line. reviewer.ts:269-273 hashes file|lineStart|title exactly as claimed. publishReviewFindings (gh-pr-write.ts:365-410) reconciles via fetchPublishedFindingIds then filters by exact id membership only; read all of gh-pr-write.ts and forge-comment-marker.ts looking for a fuzzy guard — none exists. serve.ts review-bundle route (1226-1270) confirms suppression is also exact-id. The one material overstatement: I read the full launch.ts auto-fix/re-review block (lines 120-260) and grepped launch.ts for publish/findings.json — the pipeline's own re-review after a fix commit never publishes findings, so the "common case after an auto-fix round" cannot produce GitHub duplicates; only back-to-back operator ad-hoc reviews with publish opt-in (review-actions.ts:434-451) can. That, plus the fact that the consequence is duplicated visible comments rather than dropped/invisible findings or lying terminal status, moves it from HIGH to MEDIUM per the given severity lens. The id-instability-on-line-shift design flaw and the false idempotency promise in forge-comment-marker.ts:10-15 are real.

serious.title: runCommentFix silently drops selected targets that don't match the latest local findings/bundle — no validation entry, no state, no warning
serious.detail: The parent filters the operator's selection down to fixableTargets (comment must be anchored in the freshly fetched bundle, review id must exist, finding id must exist in the NEWEST local findings.json with a file). Targets failing the filter are dropped before the worker is spawned: only fixableTargets are stored in session metrics/meta, so the worker's 'unresolved → disputed' backfill never sees them and findLatestCommentFixState has no entry for them. The UI row for the dropped target shows no status change at all. This is easy to hit: a published marker comment carries a finding id from an older review run; after a re-review the latest findings.json no longer contains that id (line shifted → new id, or reviewer didn't re-report it), so the operator-selected `finding:<id>` silently vanishes from the run. Only when EVERY target drops does the user get the NO_COMMENTS 409.
serious.evidence: comment-fix-actions.ts:284-294: `const findingIds = new Set(findLatestForgeFindings(...).findings.filter((f) => f.file).map((f) => f.id)); const fixableTargets = uniqueTargets.filter(...)`; 347 `targets: fixableTargets` (meta) and 370 metrics.targets — dropped targets never reach the worker's disputed backfill (697-702). review-targets.ts:26-28: marker comments are selected as `finding:<forgeFindingId>` where the id comes from the GitHub marker, not the latest findings.json. findLatestForgeFindings (review-actions.ts:800-863) returns only the single newest findings.json.
serious.fix: Don't drop: pass all requested targets to the worker and stamp non-matching ones disputed/failed with an explicit reason (e.g. 'finding not present in latest review run'), or return the dropped tokens in the HTTP response so the UI can mark them. For finding targets, fall back to searching older findings.json runs (or reconstruct the body from the marker comment itself).
serious.test: Call runCommentFix with one fixable comment target and one finding id absent from findings.json; assert the response/validation state contains an explicit entry for the unmatched finding rather than omitting it.

verdict.correctedDetail: Confirmed as described, with one cosmetic correction: when EVERY target drops, the NO_COMMENTS CliError maps to HTTP 400 (serve.ts:2374), not 409. The core bug is real: comment-fix-actions.ts:289-294 filters uniqueTargets to fixableTargets with no warning, no per-target record; only fixableTargets are persisted to meta.json (line 347) and session metrics (line 370), so the worker's disputed backfill (lines 697-702) — whose own comment at 563-565 says unresolved targets must be stamped 'disputed' rather than dropped silently — never sees them, validation.json has no row, findLatestCommentFixState returns nothing, and statusBadgeFor in CommentThread.tsx/FindingsRail.tsx renders no badge. The HTTP response carries only {sessionId, logStreamUrl}. The stale-id trigger is realistic: serve.ts:1257-1268 stamps forgeFindingId from the GitHub marker regardless of the latest findings.json, commentTargetToken yields finding:<oldId>, finding ids are hash(file,lineStart,title) (reviewer.ts:269) so a re-review with a shifted line or reworded title mints a new id, and findLatestForgeFindings reads only the single newest findings.json. tests/comment-fix-actions.test.ts only pins the all-targets-invalid case, not partial drops.
verdict.reasoning: I re-traced every cited hop in the actual code. (1) Parent filter: comment-fix-actions.ts:284-294 builds findingIds from findLatestForgeFindings (newest single findings.json, review-actions.ts:800-863) and silently filters; nothing between the filter and the zero-length check logs or records dropped targets. (2) Persistence: meta targets (line 347) and metrics targets (line 370) are fixableTargets only; the worker re-derives targets from metrics (line 461), so its 'unresolved -> disputed' backfill (697-702) cannot cover parent-dropped targets — and the worker's own comment (563-565) states the design intent that unresolved targets be stamped disputed 'rather than dropping them silently', which the parent violates. (3) Visibility: validation.json never gets a row, findLatestCommentFixState (1276-1339) keys off validation.json entries, CommentThread.tsx statusBadgeFor (41-50) returns null with no persisted entry, and the fix-comments route (serve.ts:2358-2360) returns only sessionId/logStreamUrl. (4) Trigger plausibility: serve.ts:1257-1268 stamps forgeFindingId purely from the GitHub marker; review-targets.ts:26-28 converts marker comments to finding:<id> tokens; ids are content hashes of file+lineStart+title (reviewer.ts:266-269), so re-reviews routinely mint new ids. (5) No guard elsewhere: tests/comment-fix-actions.test.ts covers only empty/all-invalid target rejection. Severity HIGH fits the stated lens (silent partial failure / status that misleads by omission in the operator's top-pain flow), though not BLOCKER since the GitHub thread remains visibly open and no data is lost. Only mis-statement found: 409 should be 400.

serious.title: Review/comment-fix worker killed without finalizing leaves the session 'running' forever — single-flight guard then permanently 409s all future runs for that PR
serious.detail: Both workers finalize their session row only on their own normal exit path. If the detached bun process dies un-catchably (SIGKILL, OOM, machine crash/reboot, bun panic) the row stays state='running'. reconcileExecutionSessions sweeps only purpose='execution' sessions (keyed off job rows); there is no reaper or kill route for purpose='review'/'comment-fix'. The next 'Run review'/'Fix comments' for that PR hits the single-flight query and returns REVIEW_IN_FLIGHT/FIX_IN_FLIGHT 409 referencing a dead session, with no recovery path short of manual SQLite surgery in ~/.forge. The Workbench also shows the phantom run as live indefinitely (lying status).
serious.evidence: comment-fix-actions.ts:304-320: `SELECT id FROM sessions WHERE purpose = 'comment-fix' AND state = 'running' ... → throw CliError('FIX_IN_FLIGHT', ...)`; review-actions.ts:170-186 same for 'review'. writes.ts:212-225 reconcileExecutionSessions: `WHERE sessions.purpose = 'execution' AND sessions.state = 'running' AND j.session_id = sessions.id` — review/comment-fix sessions have no job row and are never swept. grep over serve.ts shows reapers only for critiques (reapStaleCritiques, serve.ts:2673) and plan-chats; no session-kill API route exists (only /api/plans/:id/kill).
serious.fix: On serve boot (and periodically), sweep 'review'/'comment-fix' sessions whose PID is gone or whose started_at exceeds a TTL with no log growth, finalizing them failed ('worker died'); store the worker PID in metrics to make liveness checkable. Add a 'cancel/clear' action where the 409 is surfaced.
serious.test: Insert a running comment-fix session row with a dead PID/old started_at, boot the reaper, assert it finalizes failed and runCommentFix no longer throws FIX_IN_FLIGHT.

verdict.correctedDetail: Confirmed as described, with one correction: recovery does not strictly require raw SQLite surgery. The internal runner helper `forge session finish --id <session-id> --exit-code <n>` (src/cli/cmd/session.ts:19-30, 116-145) calls finalizeSession and will unstick the row. However it is documented as a bash-runner-internal record hook, is not surfaced in the 409 error hint, and requires the operator to discover the stuck session id — so there is still no discoverable operator-facing recovery path. Everything else verifies: single-flight queries (review-actions.ts:170-187, comment-fix-actions.ts:304-320) have no staleness bound or pid check; workers are detached+unref'd with no pid recorded and no signal handlers (review-actions.ts:246-254, comment-fix-actions.ts:390-396); reconcileExecutionSessions (writes.ts:212-225) joins jobs and is gated to purpose='execution' so review/comment-fix sessions are never swept; the only boot reapers are reapStaleCritiques (serve.ts:2672-2688) and reapStalePlanChats — neither touches these sessions; serve.ts:2311/2366 turns the guard into a permanent 409; activity views render the phantom 'running' row (serve.ts:1630, 1744-1746).
verdict.reasoning: Re-traced every cited path in the actual code. (1) Both single-flight guards select sessions by purpose/state='running'/prNum/repoRoot with no startedAt threshold and no process-liveness check, then throw REVIEW_IN_FLIGHT/FIX_IN_FLIGHT, mapped to HTTP 409 in serve.ts:2311/2366 with no pre-sweep in the route handlers. (2) Workers are spawned detached and unref'd; no pid is persisted (grep for pid/process.kill in both files returns nothing) and no SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers exist, so finalizeSession only runs on the worker's own normal/error exit paths — uncatchable death (SIGKILL, OOM, reboot) leaves state='running' forever. (3) reconcileExecutionSessions (writes.ts:212-225) is explicitly `WHERE sessions.purpose = 'execution' ... FROM jobs j ... j.session_id = sessions.id`; review/comment-fix sessions have no jobs row and a different purpose, so they are never reconciled. Exhaustive grep for `UPDATE sessions` and reapers confirms no other writer or sweep covers them (reapStaleCritiques sweeps critique meta files only; reapStalePlanChats sweeps plan-chat artifacts only). (4) The Workbench activity path renders review/comment-fix session rows and their running state comes straight from the never-corrected row, so the lying-status claim holds. The only inaccuracy in the claim is "no recovery short of manual SQLite surgery": the internal `forge session finish --id ... --exit-code 1` helper can finalize any session row without raw SQL, though it is undocumented as a recovery path and not hinted at in the 409, so severity HIGH stands (permanent 409 on the operator's top-pain pipeline plus indefinitely lying status, violating the F0 no-silent-failure goal).

serious.title: commitAndPush runs bare `git commit` / `git push` in the detached worker — gpg signing (1Password) or credential prompts hang the fix headlessly
serious.detail: The fixer's commit step inherits the repo/global git config. With commit.gpgsign=true backed by 1Password (the canonical trap in this project's own notes), `git commit` blocks on the signing prompt while the screen is locked; `git push` can similarly block on an interactive credential helper or host-key prompt. The worker has stdin 'ignore' but the gpg/askpass flow talks to the agent socket, so the process just hangs: session stays 'running', quality already passed, edits sit uncommitted in the worktree, and the single-flight guard blocks retries. No GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 / --no-gpg-sign / GCM-disable is set anywhere in src (grep for 'gpg' over src/ returns nothing).
serious.evidence: comment-fix-actions.ts:1259-1267: `execFileSync("git", ["-C", worktreePath, "commit", "-m", msg], { stdio: ["pipe", "inherit", "inherit"] }); execFileSync("git", ["-C", worktreePath, "push"], ...)` — no timeout, no GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0, no -c commit.gpgsign handling. Memory note 'onepassword-signing-breaks-headless' documents this exact failure mode for Forge headless runs.
serious.fix: Run git with `env: { ...env, GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT: "0" }`, a timeout on execFileSync, and either `-c gpg.program=...`/`--no-gpg-sign` behind a repo-config flag or detect signing config and fail fast with an actionable error instead of hanging.
serious.test: Configure a worktree with commit.gpgsign=true and gpg.program=/bin/false equivalent that blocks; assert commitAndPush fails within the timeout with a clear error rather than hanging.

verdict.correctedDetail: Confirmed as stated, with one weak sub-claim. commitAndPush (src/cli/cmd/comment-fix-actions.ts:1259-1267) runs bare `git commit`/`git push` via execFileSync with no timeout and no env override; grep over all non-dist TS finds zero handling of gpg/gpgsign/GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT/askpass anywhere, and resolveGhEnv (src/core/gh.ts:60-85) only injects GH_HOST/GH_TOKEN. The worker is detached (Bun.spawn stdio ["ignore",logFd,logFd] + unref, lines 390-396); the session row stays state='running' (line 362) because finalizeSession (line 788) only runs after the try block containing commitAndPush (line 745), and the single-flight guard (lines 304-320) blocks any retry for that PR while state='running'. Quality gates run before the commit (line 729), so a gpg hang leaves passing-but-uncommitted edits and a forever-'running' session with no reaper. Caveat: the `git push` credential-prompt hang is less likely in practice (gh's credential helper is non-interactive and git without a tty usually errors instead of prompting); the real headless hang is the `git commit` signing step via 1Password, exactly matching the operator's documented trap.
verdict.reasoning: Re-traced the full path: spawn site (comment-fix-actions.ts:377-417), worker body (lines 653-798), commitAndPush (lines 1259-1267), single-flight guard (lines 303-320), and resolveGhEnv (src/core/gh.ts). Verified by grep that no GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT/gpg/askpass mitigation exists anywhere in src and no git config is written into worktrees, so global commit.gpgsign=true is inherited. A blocked execFileSync in the detached worker prevents finalizeSession from ever running, leaving the session 'running' (lying status), blocking retries via the single-flight guard, with quality-passed edits stranded uncommitted. HIGH is the right severity per the given lens (hang + lying status + headless-blocking); not BLOCKER because no data is lost (edits remain in the worktree) and findings are not dropped.

areas.summary: The execution layer's supervision story is thin and the PR #64 lesson was applied only to critique: every other stream-json pipeline (launch agent, launch reviewer/fixer, ad-hoc review, comment-fix) still trusts raw exit codes, and the detached review/comment-fix workers have no pid tracking, no reaper, and a single-flight guard that permanently wedges a PR if a worker dies. Worst of all, the launch runner's set_status helper has been a silent no-op since inception due to a bash/JSON quoting bug (reproduced locally), so every failure path leaves meta.json/jobs/Workbench claiming 'running' forever — a direct violation of 'no silent failure'. The headless mandate is also breached by Forge-authored git commits that inherit gpg signing (the known 1Password trap) and by a total absence of timeouts, while the top operator pain (findings not reaching GitHub) is structurally guaranteed: publish failures are caught, logged to a transient SSE log, and the review still finalizes as 'completed'.

minor.title: Critique reaper runs only at serve boot and marks any 10-minute-old running critique failed without a liveness check
minor.detail: reapStaleCritiques is invoked once in startServer (the 60s interval reaps only plan-chats), so a critique/improve worker that dies while `forge serve` keeps running leaves the Workbench 'Improving' pill stuck until the next serve restart. Conversely, at boot the sweep marks ANY running critique older than 10 minutes failed without checking tmux liveness (the comment explains why tmux can't be trusted: the sync improve path writes a tmuxSession it never creates) — a legitimately long background critique that spans a serve restart is falsely failed in critique-meta and synced to the DB; the bash runner later overwrites meta to done, leaving the two stores temporarily contradictory.
minor.evidence: serve.ts:2672-2689: `const STALE_IMPROVE_MS = 10 * 60_000; ... if (now - startedMs < STALE_IMPROVE_MS) continue; store.writeCritiqueMeta(..., { status: "failed" ... })` with the comment 'We can't use isTmuxSessionAlive as a liveness check because the sync improve path writes a tmuxSession ... but never actually creates that tmux session'. serve.ts:2727-2736: the periodic interval calls only `reapStalePlanChats`.
minor.fix: Distinguish the two launch modes in critique-meta (e.g. `mode: 'sync'|'background'` is already in CritiqueConfig — persist it) so background critiques can use real tmux liveness, and run the sweep on the periodic timer too.
minor.test: Write a critique-meta with mode='background', a live tmux session, startedAt 20 minutes ago; run the reaper; assert it is NOT marked failed. And: kill the session, run the periodic sweep, assert it flips failed without a serve restart.

minor.title: gh failures during prompt assembly degrade silently to an empty diff — the reviewer reviews nothing and can emit a verdict anyway
minor.detail: In the ad-hoc review worker, runGhSafe catches any `gh pr view/checks/diff` failure (auth expiry, network, rate limit), logs one line, and returns "" — the reviewer prompt is then built with an empty Diff/metadata section and the run proceeds to a 'completed' review whose verdict was produced against no code. The launch runner has the same shape: `gh pr diff "$PR_NUMBER" 2>/dev/null | head -c 60000` inside the prompt heredoc inserts an empty diff on failure. Neither path checks for an empty diff before invoking the reviewer, so an auth blip turns into a confidently wrong approve/request-changes rather than a visible failure.
minor.evidence: review-actions.ts:580-594 runGhSafe: `catch (e) { ... process.stdout.write(`[forge:review-worker] gh ${args.join(" ")} failed: ${detail}\n`); return ""; }` consumed at :357-359 (`const diff = runGhSafe(["pr", "diff", ...])`) with no emptiness check before buildReviewerPrompt. launch.ts:656: `gh pr diff "$PR_NUMBER" 2>/dev/null | head -c 60000`.
minor.fix: Treat an empty `gh pr diff` as fatal for the review step (fail the session with a GH_FAIL error naming the gh command), or at minimum stamp the resulting review as 'diff unavailable' in meta/verdict handling.
minor.test: Stub gh to fail `pr diff`; run runReviewWorker; assert the session finalizes failed with an actionable error instead of completed-with-verdict.

minor.title: Plan-chat reaper SIGTERMs any turn older than 5 minutes even while it is actively streaming, and the partial reply is discarded
minor.detail: reapStalePlanChats kills in-flight plan-chat children based purely on spawn time (`now - entry.startedAt >= 5min`), with no last-activity check — yet the SSE pipeline tracks `lastWriteAt` and forwards api_retry/ping keepalives precisely because long turns are expected. A planner turn doing multi-minute repo exploration is killed mid-stream at the 5-minute mark; the close handler then sees a signal exit and deliberately does NOT persist the accumulated blocks, so the user's reply is lost and a re-send starts over. Five minutes is easily exceeded by opus-class models exploring a large repo.
minor.evidence: plan-chat.ts:1154 `const STALE_PLAN_CHAT_MS = 5 * 60_000;` and :1167-1176: `if (now - entry.startedAt < STALE_PLAN_CHAT_MS) continue; entry.child.kill("SIGTERM")` — startedAt set once at spawn (:748). Non-persistence on signal exit: plan-chat.ts:1050-1057 comment 'we do NOT persist partial blocks when claude exits non-zero or via signal'.
minor.fix: Track last stdout activity per in-flight entry (update on each data chunk) and reap on idle time, not wall-clock age; or raise the budget and emit a visible 'turn killed by reaper' SSE error naming the limit.
minor.test: Spawn a stub child that emits a line every 30s for 7 minutes; run the reaper at 6 minutes; assert an actively-emitting child is not killed (idle-based) while a silent one is.

minor.title: Every run/critique tmux session blocks on `read -p 'Press Enter to close...'` forever on headless machines
minor.detail: Both launchers start tmux with `bash '<runner>'; read -p 'Press Enter to close...'`, so after the runner exits (success or failure) the session stays alive until a human attaches and presses Enter. Headless/overnight operation accumulates one zombie tmux session per run; the only cleanup is killTmuxSession on a same-plan relaunch or the Workbench kill action. It also means tmux liveness can never be used as a 'runner finished' signal (forge wait must rely on meta status — which the set_status bug breaks).
minor.evidence: launch.ts:846-848: `tmux new-session -d -s "${tmuxSession}" -c "..." "bash '${runnerPath}'; read -p 'Press Enter to close...' "`; critique.ts:493-495 identical.
minor.fix: Keep the pane open only on failure (`bash runner || read -p ...`), or add the finished sessions to the job reaper's sweep once meta is terminal.
minor.test: Launch a trivially-succeeding runner; assert the tmux session is gone (or reaped) within N seconds of meta.json reaching 'done'.

minor.title: Job stream filters drop malformed/truncated JSON lines with a bare catch{} — truncation is indistinguishable from 'no output'
minor.detail: claudeJobStreamFilter and codexJobStreamFilter (the bun -e projections every claude/codex job pipes through) wrap JSON.parse in `catch{}` with no counter or stderr note. A truncated final line (agent killed mid-write, disk full) or a format change yields zero projected output and no diagnostic; downstream the failure surfaces as the misleading 'no fenced forge-review block in reviewer output' or an empty agent log. The TS-side readers (claude-stream.ts:84-88, codex-stream.ts:97-101) skip malformed lines the same way; only plan-chat logs them (plan-chat.ts:970-973). Low because the sidecar files are preserved for post-mortem, but a one-line stderr warning would save real debugging time.
minor.evidence: agents/index.ts:71: `rl.on('line',l=>{try{const e=JSON.parse(l);...}catch{}})` (claude filter) and :89 (codex filter). claude-stream.ts:84-88: `try { parsed = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }`.
